content:
After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Organize the Compare and Contrast Essay Side-by-Side.

Week 4 | Organize Compare and Contrast Essay Side-by-Side

Organize Compare and Contrast Essay Side-by-Side
Another option to organize a compare-and-contrast essay is by comparing one side at a time.

If you choose this approach when writing your essay, you must first decide which aspects of each topic you want to focus on. Once you have done this, you will write a thesis statement explaining how the two items are similar and different. For example, if you were comparing and contrasting apples and oranges, you might say, "Apples and oranges are both fruits that are often eaten as snacks. However, apples are typically red or green, while oranges are usually orange. Apples also tend to be tart, while oranges are sweet."

After writing your thesis statement, you will need to write an introduction paragraph that gives background information about your topic. Next, you will need to write a body paragraph for each item you are comparing and contrasting. In each body paragraph, you will need to include precise details that reinforce your thesis statement. You will need to compose a conclusion paragraph outlining your essay's main points.
